intro: Calceolaria phyllanthodes, also known as Slipper Flower, is a flowering plant species native to the Andes region in South America. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include treating various health issues such as inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Calceolaria phyllanthodes include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and as an antiseptic.
